# B-009 — Multi-tenant migration plan for `apps/dwa` and `apps/gtm`

**Status:** Phase 1 (plan) — approved scope.
**Origin:** generated by worktree agent agent-ae665ffe6a26e5dde (timed out after Phase 1); plan rescued from worktree before cleanup.

## Why this exists

The platform is multi-tenant from day one (Blueprint §6, ADR 0003,
ADR 0004). The two lifted apps (`dwa`, `gtm`) predate that rule:

- **0** of their 35+ tables carry a `tenant_id` column.
- **0** RLS policies on app data; only the spine tables in
  `infra/migrations/0001_tenancy.sql` + `0002_rls_helpers.sql` are
  protected.
- **89 source files** (124 `import` statements) reach for the raw
  `db = getDb()` client and bypass `withTenant()` — meaning even if we
  added RLS today, the app code would still need refactoring to make
  the GUC `app.tenant_id` get set per request.

This plan converts both apps to the platform tenant model in one
migration cycle.

## Scope inventory

### `@platform/tenancy` spine (NOT touched)

The 9 tables in [infra/migrations/0001_tenancy.sql](../../infra/migrations/0001_tenancy.sql)
already have `tenant_id` and RLS via
[infra/migrations/0002_rls_helpers.sql](../../infra/migrations/0002_rls_helpers.sql):
`tenant`, `tenant_member`, `tenant_audit`, `system_audit`,
`tenant_quota_counter`, `billing_meter_event`, `billing_meter_daily`,
`event_outbox`, `event_dispatch_log`. Constraint: do not modify these
in this migration.

### DWA — `apps/dwa/lib/db/schema.ts` (19 tables)

| Table | Decision | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| `user` | **excluded** from `tenant_id`; planned move to `@platform/identity` | Lucia auth identity; cross-tenant by definition for SSO/RBAC. Migrating later under `@platform/identity` not B-009. |
| `session` | **excluded** | Lucia's session table; references `user`. Same reason as above. |
| `profile` | tenant_id | per-user wellness profile; tenant-scoped per Blueprint §6.1 |
| `mood_entry` | tenant_id | tenant-scoped per Blueprint §6.1 |
| `coach_session` | tenant_id | tenant-scoped |
| `lesson_feedback` | tenant_id | tenant-scoped |
| `forum_bookmark` | tenant_id | tenant-scoped |
| `distress_event` | tenant_id | tenant-scoped (HIPAA audit log) |
| `provider_profile` | tenant_id | tenant-scoped |
| `provider_patient` | tenant_id | tenant-scoped |
| `patient_assignment` | tenant_id | tenant-scoped |
| `provider_invite` | tenant_id | tenant-scoped |
| `content_embedding` | tenant_id | tenant-scoped (RAG corpus is per-tenant) |
| `moderation_log` | tenant_id | tenant-scoped (audit) |
| `ai_classification_event` | tenant_id | tenant-scoped (audit) |
| `forum_topic_classification` | tenant_id | tenant-scoped |
| `content_quality_score` | tenant_id | tenant-scoped |
| `content_atomization_tag` | tenant_id | tenant-scoped |
| `clinical_component_data` | tenant_id | tenant-scoped (PHI) |

Total tables migrated for DWA: **17**.

### GTM — `apps/gtm/lib/db/schema.ts` (35 tables)

| Table | Decision | Rationale |

| `user` | **excluded** | Same as DWA. |

Total tables migrated for GTM: **32**. One excluded as system-only
(`book_search_index`).

### `apps/gtm/apps/forms` sub-app

The forms sub-app reuses GTM's `lib/db.ts` (a one-off file).
Out-of-scope for this migration except for the fact that we
re-route `@/lib/db` consumers to use `withTenantApp`. Will be
handled when the sub-app is folded into mainline GTM.

## Architectural decisions

### D-1 — Per-app `withTenantApp` wrapper, NOT schema consolidation

`@platform/tenancy.withTenant(ctx, fn)` returns a `tx` typed against
the platform-spine schema. App tables are not in that schema, and
moving them there would (a) cross the engine-admission rule (only
two consumers, no third committed) and (b) bloat the platform
package surface.

Decision: each app gets its own thin wrapper in
`apps/<name>/lib/db/with-tenant.ts` that:

1. Opens a transaction on the **app's existing Drizzle pool** (so
   the app's typed schema is preserved).
2. Issues the same `SET LOCAL ROLE platform_tenant` / `platform_system`
   and `SELECT set_config('app.tenant_id', …, true)` that
   `@platform/tenancy.withTenant` does.
3. Exposes the transaction typed as `AppTenantTx` so existing code
   using `db.select().from(schema.thing)` becomes
   `tx.select().from(schema.thing)` with no other changes.

Both `@platform/tenancy.withTenant` and the per-app wrapper hit the
same Postgres database via the same `DATABASE_URL`. RLS doesn't care
which pool drove the transaction — it only sees the role and
`app.tenant_id` GUC.

### D-2 — `user` and `session` stay app-local for now

Lucia's `user`/`session` tables are NOT migrated to `tenant_id` in
this work:

- A user can in principle belong to multiple tenants
  (e.g. internal staff with admin in DWA + GTM). Pinning `tenant_id`
  on `user` would force one-to-one.
- `tenant_member` already models the user→tenant relation per
  Blueprint §6.1.
- `@platform/identity` is the planned home for these tables; B-009
  doesn't pre-empt that work.

`user`/`session` reads stay outside `withTenantApp` and use the
existing `getDb()` (or the new `getAppDb()` direct pool) only for
auth-flow paths. **Audit caveat:** until `@platform/identity` lands,
`user`/`session` are not RLS-protected. This is a known gap.

### D-3 — `tenant_id NOT NULL` after backfill — NO nullable column phase

The brief says treat existing dev/test rows as throwaway. The
chosen path is: **wipe app-data tables on dev/staging during
migration**, then add `tenant_id NOT NULL`. No nullable transition
column; no backfill code. Production Dokploy deployments are
**source-repo-only and out of scope** (see source-repo-backports.md);
the monorepo apps have not yet been deployed and have no production
data.

If someone has dev data they want to keep, they wipe and re-seed via
the new tenant-onboarding tool.

### D-4 — RLS policy shape

Mirrors the helpers in `0002_rls_helpers.sql`:

```sql
ALTER TABLE <table> ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
ALTER TABLE <table> FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;

CREATE POLICY <table>_tenant_isolation ON <table>
  FOR ALL TO platform_tenant
  USING      (tenant_id = current_setting('app.tenant_id', true)::uuid)
  WITH CHECK (tenant_id = current_setting('app.tenant_id', true)::uuid);

CREATE POLICY <table>_system_bypass ON <table>
  FOR ALL TO platform_system
  USING (true) WITH CHECK (true);
```

Clause order: `FOR <op>` before `TO <role>` (B-005 — Postgres
grammar). The migration uses the existing
`apply_tenant_policies(target_table)` helper from
`0002_rls_helpers.sql` to avoid duplication.

### D-5 — Indexes on `(tenant_id, …)`

For each migrated table we add:
1. A `(tenant_id)` index if the table was lookup-by-PK/FK only.
2. A `(tenant_id, user_id)` index where the existing access pattern
   was `WHERE user_id = $1`.
3. A `(tenant_id, <date>)` index where the pattern is range-by-time.

The planner uses `tenant_id` as the leading column once RLS forces
the predicate, so without these indexes every query becomes a seq
scan on tenant.

### D-6 — Tenant onboarding: extend `seed-tenant.ts` + add admin API per app

- **`tools/tenancy/seed-tenant.ts`** (existing CLI) — extended with
  `--scaffold-app dwa|gtm|both` flag. When set, calls into
  per-app scaffolding hooks (see below).
- **`apps/dwa/app/api/admin/tenants/route.ts`** — new POST endpoint;
  authenticated as `super_admin`; idempotent by slug. Calls the same
  scaffolding hook.
- **`apps/gtm/app/api/admin/tenants/route.ts`** — symmetric.

Per-app scaffolding for a new tenant:

- DWA: nothing required at row level. App reads default content from
  the manifest, not from a settings table. New tenants are
  immediately usable once `tenant` + `tenant_member` are in place.
- GTM: nothing required at row level for the same reason. Future
  per-tenant content (e.g. starter pods) can be seeded via the
  scaffolding hook when that comes.

For B-009 the scaffolding hooks are **no-ops**; the entry point
exists so future per-app onboarding has a stable API. Design intent
documented; implementation deliberately empty.

### D-7 — `withSystemAdmin` candidates

Routes that should NOT pin `app.tenant_id` and should run as
`platform_system`:

- `apps/dwa/app/api/cron/purge-deleted-accounts/route.ts` — cron
  sweeps soft-deleted accounts across all tenants.
- `apps/dwa/app/api/admin/providers/**` — super-admin views all
  providers across tenants.
- `apps/gtm/app/api/admin/forms/**` — super-admin views form
  submissions across tenants.
- `apps/gtm/app/api/webhook/polar/route.ts` — Polar webhook resolves
  tenant from `userId` → membership lookup → `withTenant` for the
  resolved tenant. Two-step: first resolve, then pin.
- `apps/gtm/app/api/webhook/attio/route.ts` — same shape as Polar.
- `apps/gtm/app/api/webhooks/nodebb/route.ts` — same.
- `apps/gtm/app/api/admin/daily-digest/route.ts` — cron, all tenants.
- `apps/gtm/app/api/admin/weekly-progress/route.ts` — cron.
- `apps/gtm/app/api/admin/readiness-followup/route.ts` — cron.
- `apps/gtm/app/api/book/search/route.ts` — `book_search_index` is
  system-owned; reads via `withSystemAdmin`.

All other routes use the per-tenant `withTenantApp`.

### D-8 — Auth flows (signup, signin)

`signin`/`signup` routes need `user` + `session` access (excluded
from RLS) and may also need to insert a `profile` row (RLS-scoped).
Pattern:

1. Read `user` + create/validate `session` via `getAppDb()` (raw
   pool, no `tenant_id`).
2. Resolve tenant via `requireTenantContext(request, { userId })`.
3. Insert `profile` (and any tenant-scoped row) inside
   `withTenantApp({ tenantId, userId }, async tx => …)`.

For dev mode where there's no resolvable tenant from host, we fall
back to a "bootstrap-dev" tenant (slug `bootstrap-dev`) that the new
`pnpm seed-tenant --slug bootstrap-dev` invocation creates.

## Implementation order

| Phase | Deliverable | File(s) |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | This plan | `docs/Paas/B-009-migration-plan.md` |
| 2 | SQL migration | `infra/migrations/0003_app_tenancy.sql` |
| 3 | Drizzle schema updates | `apps/{dwa,gtm}/lib/db/schema.ts` |
| 4 | Per-app `withTenantApp` wrapper | `apps/{dwa,gtm}/lib/db/with-tenant.ts` |
| 5 | Tenant onboarding | `tools/tenancy/seed-tenant.ts` (extend), `apps/{dwa,gtm}/app/api/admin/tenants/route.ts` (new) |
| 6 | Leak harness extension | `packages/testing/src/appTablesLeakHarness.ts` |
| 7 | Exemplar route refactor | ~5 routes per app, demonstrating the pattern |
| 8 | Bulk import refactor | remaining ~80 sites |
| 9 | Verification | `pnpm -r typecheck`; `pnpm --filter @platform/testing test` (with `TEST_DATABASE_URL`) |
| 10 | Doc updates | `CLAUDE.md`, `docs/Paas/bug-patterns.md`, `docs/Paas/source-repo-backports.md` |

Phase 8 is the bulk of the work and is **mechanical**. Each call
site is a 3-line change: replace `const db = getDb(); … db.select(…)`
with `await withTenantApp(ctx, async (tx) => tx.select(…))`. The
context shape is uniform (`{ tenantId, userId }` from
`requireTenantContext`).

## Verification gates

- `pnpm -r typecheck` clean (modulo pre-existing static-image misses
  baseline of 165 + 157).
- New SQL migration applies cleanly on a fresh Postgres with 0001 +
  0002 already applied.
- Extended leak harness asserts cross-tenant isolation for at least
  one tenant-scoped table per app (chosen: `mood_entry` for DWA,
  `roleplay_session` for GTM — both are user-scoped writes that
  represent the largest class).
- Manual spot-check of 5+ refactored routes per app: confirm reads
  go through `withTenantApp`, GUC is set, cross-tenant query returns
  zero rows.

## Out-of-scope

- `user`/`session` migration to `@platform/identity`.
- Source-repo backports (mental-health-education-platform-main,
  customer-acquisition-academy-vps don't have the platform spine —
  this work is monorepo-only; documented in
  `docs/Paas/source-repo-backports.md`).
- The `apps/gtm/apps/forms` sub-app and its private `lib/db.ts`.
- Production data backfill (none exists yet for the monorepo apps).
